rows.notes: Top-line title is centered and very clear; no monetary columns present. Lettering legible and unambiguous.

rows.description: a secundo die Novembris 1774 ad eundem
rows.notes: Second header line immediately under title. The year '1774' is clearly visible near 'Novembris'. No amounts present.
rows.section_header: a secundo die Novembris 1774 ad eundem

rows.notes: Third header line clear: 'diem revolutum 1775'. No monetary values.
Section heading centered under ruled line. Handwriting legible; no amounts on this line.
Amounts '30 0 0' are neatly aligned under the three right-hand columns (pounds/shillings/pence). Vertical guides clear; digits unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of Gwinear.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of Gwinear, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.description: South Newington Rect: pro dim: An.d Mic:
rows.notes: Line reads '30 0 0' clearly. Description handwriting slightly flourished but unambiguous in context of parish name; numeric columns well aligned.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of South Newington, due at Michaelmas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of South Newington, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings '88 5' are clear. Pence appears as '1¾' (three‑quarters of a penny) — the small '¾' sign is present but slightly stylized; column alignment supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from the rectory of Long Wittenham.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from the rectory of Long Wittenham, amounting to £88 5s 1 3/4d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings '28 11' clear. Pence reads as '10¾' (ten and three‑quarters pence) — the fraction mark is present but slightly faint; columns well aligned.
rows.english_description: Biennial receipt from the Kidlington mills.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial receipt from the Kidlington mills, amounting to £28 11s 10 3/4d.

rows.notes: Numbers read as '07 5 02' with pence '02' (2d). Alignment with vertical rules and the identical pattern on the following similar row support reading of '2' pence.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from a tenant (Sadler) at Garsington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from a tenant (Sadler) at Garsington, amounting to £7 5s 2d.

rows.notes: Mirrors the previous row numerically: '07 5 02'. The pence digits align with the same column and match the shape on the Garsington line (clear '2').
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Henry Pritchett (same place as the previous entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Henry Pritchett, amounting to £7 5s 2d.

rows.description: Tintinhull Napper pro trien: et dim:
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings '20 7' are clear. Pence appears as '9½' (nine and a half pence) — a small half‑penny loop present after the '9'. Fraction reading fairly clear though the loop is slightly light.
rows.english_description: Multi-year arrear payment from Napper of Tintinhull covering three years and an additional half-year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrear payment from Napper of Tintinhull for three years and a half, amounting to £20 7s 9 1/2d.

rows.notes: Amounts '02 3 6' are crisply written and well aligned in the pence column (6d). The parish name has a small abbreviation but the numbers are unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Annual receipt of the tenths (ecclesiastical tenth) from South Newington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual receipt of the tenths from South Newington, amounting to £2 3s 6d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings '05 13' clear. Pence shows '6' with a small half‑penny loop (interpreted as 6½d). The halfpenny loop is somewhat faint but present; alignment supports reading.
rows.english_description: Annual rent from the estate at Bensington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent from Bensington, amounting to £5 13s 6 1/2d.

rows.notes: Clear '03 0 0' in the three numeric columns. Description short and legible.
rows.english_description: Payment for Bampton covering a three-year period (triennium).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for Bampton for the triennium, amounting to £3 0s 0d.

rows.description: Back Gate, Draper, pro An. & pfter parte
rows.notes: Numeric trio '11 5 0' clearly written and aligned. The description contains abbreviations ('An. & pfter parte') but is legible enough to transcribe as shown.
rows.english_description: Annual rent from Back Gate paid by tenant Draper, including part payment for a subsequent period.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent from Back Gate paid by Draper, amounting to £11 5s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds/shillings/pence read as '13 6 8'. The pence '8' is slightly crowded by nearby ink/shadow but aligns with the pence column and is clearly shaped.
rows.english_description: Biennial payment from the property on Lad Lane at Stanforth.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment from Lad Lane, Stanforth, amounting to £13 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Values '03 6 8' align under columns and match the style of the preceding row; small shadow at left does not affect numeric legibility.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment from Greenhow, amounting to £3 6s 8d.

rows.description: Sir Gregory Turner, 3 closes pro Anno
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings '16 7' clear. Pence reads as '10¾' (ten and three‑quarters pence) — the '¾' symbol is visible though slightly compressed; column alignment supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Annual rent from Sir Gregory Turner for three closes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent from Sir Gregory Turner for three closes, amounting to £16 7s 10 3/4d.

rows.notes: Amounts read '01 18 0' clearly. The parenthetical note '(super Franklin)' is a best reading of the cursive; wording transcribed as appears though parenthetical text is somewhat compressed.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Giles Hall (noted as included with sums).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Giles Hall (sums included), amounting to £1 18s 0d.

rows.notes: Signature or clerk's name at bottom right. Handwriting is stylized and partially obscured by a light stain/shadow; transcription 'Sayer olin Myner' is uncertain. No monetary columns on this line.
